title: Average precipitation in depth (mm per year)
definition: Average precipitation is the long-term average in depth (over space and time) of annual precipitation in the country. Precipitation is defined as any kind of water that falls from clouds as a liquid or a solid.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: FAOSTAT, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), publisher: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for average precipitation.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Colombia, Papua New Guinea, Solomon Islands, while the lightest include Egypt, Arab Rep., Libya, Saudi Arabia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the average precipitation pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Average precipitation may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
